name: Third Eye of Shiva
description: The Third Eye of Shiva, located on his forehead, is a powerful symbol of wisdom, insight, and destructive energy in Hindu mythology. It represents Shiva's ability to see beyond the superficial and perceive the ultimate truth. This eye is not a physical organ in the conventional sense but a spiritual center of power and awareness. When opened, it emits intense flames capable of incinerating anything that stands in its path. The Third Eye is a manifestation of Shiva's wrath and is often invoked when he needs to destroy evil or restore balance to the universe. Its opening signifies the complete annihilation of illusion and ignorance, leading to enlightenment and liberation. The eye's destructive power is not inherently malevolent; rather, it is a force for purification and transformation, burning away negativity to pave the way for renewal. The Third Eye serves as a constant reminder of the interconnectedness of creation and destruction, highlighting the cyclical nature of existence and the importance of discerning truth from falsehood. It embodies Shiva's role as both destroyer and regenerator, emphasizing the necessary balance between these opposing forces in the cosmic order. The power of the Third Eye is also connected to Shiva's ascetic nature, representing his control over his senses and desires, and his ability to focus his energy for profound spiritual insight. It's a reminder to look beyond the obvious, question assumptions, and seek deeper understanding. The imagery associated with the Third Eye is prominent in Hindu art and iconography, serving as a visual representation of Shiva's divine power and his ability to perceive the hidden realities of the universe. The eye can also represent the ability to see past the limitations of the physical senses and perceive the true nature of reality.
subtitle: Wisdom, Insight, and Destructive Power
origin: Inherent to Shiva, manifestation of his divine power

keyMyths.title: Burning of Kama
keyMyths.description: When Kama, the god of love, disturbed Shiva's meditation, Shiva opened his Third Eye and incinerated Kama to ashes. This demonstrates the Third Eye's destructive power when provoked.

keyMyths.title: Destruction of Tripura
keyMyths.description: Shiva used the power of his Third Eye to destroy the three demon cities of Tripura, which were causing chaos and destruction in the universe. This highlights its role in restoring cosmic balance.

sources.author: Kramrisch, Stella
sources.description: Discusses the symbolism and significance of Shiva's Third Eye in Hindu art and philosophy.
sources.text: Discusses the symbolism and significance of Shiva's Third Eye in Hindu art and philosophy.

sources.author: Doniger, Wendy
sources.description: Explores the themes of destruction and creation associated with Shiva, including the power of his Third Eye.
